metadata.physical_address: Polymer Science Program, Department of Chemistry, University of Massachusetts Lowell, One University Avenue, Lowell, Massachusetts 01854, USA

conditions.evidence_text: LCCC was conducted on a Waters HPLC system with Waters 2695 separation module at a flow rate of 0.5 mL/min. For UV/RI system, a Waters 2414 Refractive Index detector at 30[◦] C was used along with a Waters 2996 Photodiode Array detector operating at A = 254 nm. 10 we L samples of 0.5 wt% polymer solutions were injected for UV/RI system. All measurements were carried out at a constant column temperature of 35[◦] C and constant sample temperature of 30[◦] C. One reversed phase C18 chromatography column (YMC HPLC COLUMN) with average particle size = 5 we m, pore diameters = 120 A i][˚] and column size (Inner diameter × Length) = 4.6 mm × 250 mm was used. HPLC grade THF and methanol were used to prepare the mobile phase and the solution of the polymers. The critical solvent composition for PIB was established as THF/methanol 80.5/19.5 (w/w).
conditions.notes: The critical condition was established for PIB samples, allowing separation based on functionality independent of molecular weight.
conditions.physical_address: Polymer Science Program, Department of Chemistry, University of Massachusetts Lowell, One University Avenue, Lowell, Massachusetts 01854, USA

conditions.analyte_polymer: PIB-diol, PIB-diallyl, PIB-monool, PIB-dichloride, PIB-diolefin, PIBOL014
conditions.critical_condition_basis: Elution independent of molecular weight for identical chemical functionality
conditions.evidence_text: The critical solvent composition for PIB was established as THF/methanol 80.5/19.5 (w/w). ... According to Fig. 6 in THF/methanol 80.5/19.5 (w/w) PIB samples with identical chemical functionality eluted at the same position, independently of their MW.
conditions.notes: RI detector used for quantitative analysis; ELS detector also used but noted as non-linear for quantification.
conditions.physical_address: Polymer Science Program, Department of Chemistry, University of Massachusetts Lowell, One University Avenue, Lowell, Massachusetts 01854, USA

conditions.evidence_text: LCCC was conducted on a Waters HPLC system with Waters 2695 separation module at a flow rate of 0.5 mL/min. For UV/RI system, a Waters 2414 Refractive Index detector at 30[◦] C was used along with a Waters 2996 Photodiode Array detector operating at A = 254 nm. 10 we L samples of 0.5 wt% polymer solutions were injected for UV/RI system. All measurements were carried out at a constant column temperature of 35[◦] C and constant sample temperature of 30[◦] C. One reversed phase C18 chromatography column (YMC HPLC COLUMN) with average particle size = 5 we m, pore diameters = 120 A i][˚] and column size (Inner diameter × Length) = 4.6 mm × 250 mm was used. HPLC grade THF and methanol were used to prepare the mobile phase and the solution of the polymers. The solvents were freshly distilled before use to avoid any degassing related issue. The critical solvent composition for PIB was established as THF/methanol 80.5/19.5 (w/w). Premixing of the mobile phase by weight is necessary for a constant and exact composition. Samples were prepared by dissolving polymer in THF/methanol 80.5/19.5 (w/w).
conditions.physical_address: Polymer Science Program, Department of Chemistry, University of Massachusetts Lowell, One University Avenue, Lowell, Massachusetts 01854, USA

conditions.evidence_text: LCCC Fractionation of Ring PS. For fractionation of ring PS, two C18 bonded silica columns (Nucleosil C18, 250 × 9.8 mm, 100 Å pore, 5 μm and Nucleosil C18, 250 × 9.8 mm, 300 Å pore, 5 μm) and a mixed eluent of CH2Cl2/CH3CN (58/42, v/v, Samchun, HPLC grade) at a flow rate of 2 mL/min were used. The temperature of the columns was set at 19 °C at which different molar mass linear PS precursors coelute.

conditions.evidence_text: At the composition of 57/43 (v/v) CH2Cl2 /CH3CN, all the PS standards are eluted as a single peak. This is the chromatographic critical point or the adsorption-size exclusion transition point where the enthalpic and entropic terms in Eq. (2) compensate each other.
conditions.notes: Critical condition for polystyrene separation.
conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry and Polymer Research Institute, POSTECH, Pohang, 790-784 KOREA

conditions.critical_condition_basis: Chromatographic critical point where enthalpic and entropic terms compensate, resulting in loss of separation selectivity according to molecular weight
conditions.evidence_text: At the composition of 57/43 (v/v) CH2Cl2 /CH3CN, all the PS standards are eluted as a single peak. This is the chromatographic critical point or the adsorption-size exclusion transition point where the enthalpic and entropic terms in Eq. (2) compensate each other. This compensation results in the loss of separation selectivity according to the polymer molecular weight, and all the PS standards are eluted near the injection solvent peak position regardless of their molecular weights.
conditions.notes: Also observed at 30.5 C with fixed composition 57/43 in Fig 3.
conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry and Polymer Research Institute, POSTECH, Pohang, 790-784 KOREA

conditions.evidence_text: Figure 1 shows the elution behavior of the 1,4-PI samples as a function of eluent composition. It can be seen that indeed near-critical conditions can be achieved by using butanone/cyclohexane mixtures as the mobile phase. If the composition of the two solvents is 92/8 vol %, these conditions are obtained for a temperature of 29 C. In this case all 1,4-PI samples elute at the same retention volume irrespective of their molar masses.
conditions.notes: Critical conditions established for 1,4-PI with mobile phase ratio 92:8 vol % butanone:cyclohexane at 29°C.
